_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the release skill: does a real `/charness:release` run honor its central claim — route to its references at the point of need? release ships a planner (plan_release_run.py, mandatory bootstrap: SKILL.md says 'run the planner before mutation... read every required_reads entry'), so RCF is derived from the planner's UNCONDITIONAL required_reads (lens 1), not prose. plan_release_run_packets.required_reads emits index.md + version-policy.md + critique-boundary.md + publication-boundary.md UNCONDITIONALLY, and appends adapter-contract.md (only if the adapter is missing/invalid/warning-bearing), install-refresh.md (only if the adapter declares post_publish_install_refresh), and real-host-proof.md (only if the adapter declares real_host_required_surfaces/path_globs) CONDITIONALLY. The prior fixture had a five-way lens-1 inversion: it tagged adapter-contract.md and install-refresh.md as engage-always RCF though the planner appends them only conditionally, and tagged index.md as on-demand though the planner requires it unconditionally. The corrected RCF is the unconditional set minus index.md: version-policy.md (the bump rubric the planner pointer does not inline), critique-boundary.md (the publish-gate rule: --execute refuses unless --critique-artifact XOR --critique-blocked), and publication-boundary.md (the irreversible-boundary rule: tag push is not public verification; record a distinct-channel verdict). index.md is dissolved from RCF by lens 7: the planner lists it unconditionally so a planner-driven run opens it, but its content IS the manual reference map the planner's own required_reads output already provides — redundant, engage-always-not-RCF (claimRole planner-briefed-disclosure-map). The three conditional reads are on-demand keyed to their adapter triggers; install-surface.md is a legacy compatibility pointer (not a planner read). Bare /charness:release stalls (no release goal/subject); the prompt pins a CONCRETE bump-and-publish objective (lens 2 + lens 8) so the planner emits the unconditional reads and the run reaches them. CAPTURE CONTEXT (non-claim, lens 5, like issue / setup greenfield): a faithful live capture TAGS, PUSHES, and PUBLISHES a GitHub release — an external irreversible side effect the Cautilus sandbox does not contain — so a clean capture needs a disposable repo/remote or stops before the publish helper's mutation; the reference routing under test is reachable in the planner/plan phase before any tag or publish. Fixture-only: the planner already gates each read correctly; the fixture just did not follow it (the index.md redundancy in the planner output is harmless, not a skill defect). declaredReferences is the full skill-local set (coverage); shared refs (fresh-eye-subagent-review.md, closeout-discipline.md) are not skill-local so they are not declared. referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract. thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget.
prompt: /charness:release 다음 릴리스를 준비해줘 — 변경의 user-visible 효과에 맞는 가장 가벼운 정직한 semver bump(patch/minor/major)을 근거와 함께 정하고, mutation 전에 standalone critique 증거를 확보했는지 먼저 확인하고, tag/push 완료가 public 릴리스 검증과 같지 않다는 전제로 public surface가 distinct channel로 verified인지와 남은 risk를 정직하게 판정해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: The planner appends this read only when the adapter is missing, invalid, or warning-bearing (plan_release_run_packets.required_reads); a representative run against a valid adapter never opens it, so the prior engage-always-RCF tag was a lens-1 over-classification.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: When the planner reports the adapter is missing, invalid, or warning-bearing and must be scaffolded or repaired.

critique-boundary.md.rationale: Unconditional planner required_read; SKILL.md step 4 also routes here explicitly. It owns the publish-gate rule (publish_release.py --execute refuses unless exactly one of --critique-artifact / --critique-blocked is present, gated before any bump/tag/push) — the rule content the planner's required_reads pointer does not inline, so a representative release run is forced to open it.

index.md.rationale: The planner lists index.md unconditionally so a planner-driven run opens it, but its content is the manual reference map ('use this index when browsing manually; the run planner owns progressive disclosure') — the same disclosure the planner's own required_reads output already provides, so it carries no unique load-bearing content: engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 7, the planner briefs the disclosure).

install-refresh.md.rationale: The planner appends this read only when the adapter declares a post_publish_install_refresh command; a release whose adapter declares no post-publish refresh never opens it, so the prior engage-always-RCF tag was a lens-1 over-classification.
install-refresh.md.trigger: When the adapter declares post_publish_install_refresh (a post-publish maintainer install-refresh command).

install-surface.md.rationale: A legacy compatibility pointer that redirects to publication-boundary.md / install-refresh.md / real-host-proof.md; it is not a planner read, and new runs use the concept-specific files instead.
install-surface.md.trigger: When a maintainer consults legacy documentation or older release records that reference install-surface concepts.

publication-boundary.md.rationale: Unconditional planner required_read; SKILL.md step 7 also routes here explicitly. It owns the irreversible-boundary rule (tag push / workflow completion is not public release verification; record a distinct-channel behavioral verdict, never a second gh release view as the distinct channel) — load-bearing content the planner pointer does not inline, so a representative run is forced to open it.

real-host-proof.md.rationale: The planner appends this read only when the adapter declares real_host_required_surfaces or real_host_required_path_globs; a release slice that touches no such seam never opens it.
real-host-proof.md.trigger: When the adapter declares real_host_required_surfaces or real_host_required_path_globs that apply to the current release slice.

version-policy.md.rationale: Unconditional planner required_read; SKILL.md step 5 chooses the bump against it. It owns the patch/minor/major rubric plus the guardrail (if the bump level is debatable, say why — do not silently turn a human judgment call into a hidden default) — the judgment content the planner pointer and bump_version.py (which only computes the number from a chosen part) do not supply, so a representative run is forced to open it.
